title: echinochloa crus-galli var. oryzicola
intro: Echinochloa crus-galli var. oryzicola, also known as barnyard grass, is a type of weed that belongs to the grass family and is widely distributed in tropical and subtropical regions. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal medicine, particularly in Southeast Asia, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Echinochloa crus-galli var. oryzicola include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and boost the immune system. Echinochloa crus-galli var. oryzicola is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and decoctions, and its roots, leaves, and seeds are typically used for medicinal purposes.
